J1.basis: this site is mid-pivot. It rebranded from "InboxZap" (an email-cleanup tool) to "Lumina" (an AI meeting-notes assistant)... GSC shows /blog/post-1 through /post-12 (old InboxZap-topic posts)
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot from the live product, treats the GSC inbox-cleanup queries as legacy demand from the old product, and frames every fix around the current meeting-assistant reality.

J2.basis: GSC shows /blog/post-1 through /post-12 (old InboxZap-topic posts) still getting thousands of impressions at page-4/5 rankings... added 301 redirects from those URLs to /blog instead of leaving them to soft-404
J2.rationale: References real aggregate GSC impressions/positions and makes one disposition (blanket 301 to the blog index), but does not walk the 12 posts asset-by-asset, cite per-post numbers, or distinguish harvest/repurpose vs redirect vs sunset — one uniform rule rather than data-justified per-asset decisions.

J3.basis: added 301 redirects from those URLs to /blog instead of leaving them to soft-404, to recover some of that equity rather than waste it... wrote 3 posts on-topic for the current product
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — doesn't chase dead inbox-cleanup content and doesn't torch legacy equity, instead 301-redirecting to preserve it while building the meeting-assistant cluster; weakened only by redirecting to the generic /blog index rather than topically-relevant successors and thin explicit sequencing.

J1.basis: this site is mid-pivot. It rebranded from "InboxZap" (an email-cleanup tool) to "Lumina" (an AI meeting-notes assistant), but the technical SEO surface never caught up
J1.rationale: Explicitly nails the pivot, correctly labels GSC inbox-cleanup posts as legacy/off-topic and the live site as the current product, and frames every fix around the meeting-assistant reality. Just shy of 10 for not fully stating the repo-is-stale angle.

J2.basis: GSC shows /blog/post-1 through /post-12 (old InboxZap-topic posts) still getting thousands of impressions at page-4/5 rankings. Since that content no longer exists, added 301 redirects from those URLs to /blog
J2.rationale: References the data only in aggregate and applies one blanket 301 (all 12 posts → /blog) rather than per-asset harvest/repurpose/keep/sunset dispositions grounded in each URL's impressions/position — better than ignoring, but not the asset-by-asset walk the anchor rewards.

J3.basis: added 301 redirects... to recover some of that equity rather than waste it... wrote 3 posts on-topic for the current product
J3.rationale: Clearly avoids both failure modes — preserves legacy equity via redirects while building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine — but the crude redirect-all-to-/blog bridge (topical mismatch weakens equity transfer) keeps it short of a fully sequenced transition.

J1.basis: this site is mid-pivot. It rebranded from "InboxZap" (an email-cleanup tool) to "Lumina" (an AI meeting-notes assistant), but the technical SEO surface never caught up
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads the live site as the meeting assistant and the GSC-indexed post-1..12 as legacy off-topic email content, and frames every fix around the new product. Falls just short of 10 only because it doesn't lay out a forward strategy — it's remediation-framed.

J2.basis: GSC shows `/blog/post-1` through `/post-12`... added 301 redirects from those URLs to `/blog` instead of leaving them to soft-404, to recover some of that equity
J2.rationale: Makes one blanket disposition (301 all 12 to the blog index) citing only aggregate 'thousands of impressions at page-4/5' rather than walking the inventory asset-by-asset with real per-post numbers or harvest/repurpose calls. It's a real, executable action grounded loosely in the data, but not the per-asset decisioning the question rewards.

J3.basis: added 301 redirects... to recover some of that equity rather than waste it... wrote 3 posts on-topic for the current product
J3.rationale: Cleanly avoids both failure modes — doesn't chase inbox content, doesn't torch equity silently — by redirecting legacy URLs and shipping a new meeting-assistant cluster. But the transition is crude (all legacy funneled to a generic /blog with no topical successors to inherit relevance) and the tension is stated only implicitly, so it lands a workable but unrefined middle path.
